entities.name: CHICKPEA
entities.description: The crop variety Annigeri used in the study to test collar rot control.

entities.name: COLLAR ROT
entities.description: A disease caused by Sclerotium rolfsii affecting chickpea.

entities.name: SCLEROTIUM ROLFSII
entities.description: The fungal pathogen responsible for causing collar rot in chickpea.

entities.name: PSEUDOMONAS FLUORESCENS
entities.description: Bacterial strains CP 8-2 and CP 8-3 used as antagonists to control collar rot.

entities.name: THIRAM
entities.description: A fungicide used in combination with Pseudomonas fluorescens for disease control.

entities.name: ANDHRA PRADESH
entities.description: The state in India where field experiments were conducted.

entities.name: INDIA
entities.description: The country where the research was conducted.

entities.name: IN VITRO STUDIES
entities.description: Laboratory studies used to evaluate the effect of thiram on P. fluorescens growth.

entities.name: GREENHOUSE EXPERIMENTS
entities.description: Controlled environment studies to test the efficacy of treatments against collar rot.

entities.name: FIELD EXPERIMENTS
entities.description: Studies conducted in the field to test the efficacy of treatments against collar rot.
